Normalized status (returning, ended, canceled, planned, ...) — drives list-card badges

fetched_at.description: Most recent source fetch backing this answer.

found.description: False = the community databases have no markers (yet); misses are re-checked more often than hits.

sources_failed.description: Sources whose last fetch errored — their markers (if any) are the previous good data.

sources_ok.description: Sources whose last fetch succeeded (a successful fetch may still carry zero markers).

kind.description: The doc's precise kind (movie, tv, anime, talk_show, miniseries, documentary, reality, person, artist, book)

alt_titles.description: Every known alternative name for this entity — locale translations, romanizations, native script, aliases — collected across every contributing provider and deduped case-insensitively. Empty when the upstream(s) have no variants. Clients should match their query against name plus every alt_title.

enriched.description: True when the row is already in our DB; Slug is then populated.

id.description: Canonical '<provider>:<value>' key — pass to /api/v1/{type}/{id} to fetch (enriches on miss)

sources.description: All providers that contributed to this row after cross-provider dedup

end_ms.description: Omitted = runs to end of media (credits/preview markers may be open-ended).

submissions.description: Community submission/vote count backing this marker (SkipMe.db only).

A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

crew.description: Distinct crew names across every title's crew[]

total_titles.description: Enriched docs across every collection (incl. people/artists/books)

alt_titles.description: Flat union of every known alternative title (locale translations, romanizations, native script, regional AKAs). Computed at response time from the rich payload titles/akas arrays.

info.description:
Metadata service for Heya — aggregates film, TV, anime, music, and book metadata from multiple upstream sources into one normalized document per title.

Caches everything, rate-limits per source, and produces a single `heya` document per entity that merges all upstream data into one JSON blob.

**Read-only by design** — this HTTP API never exposes endpoints that modify, refresh, reindex, or delete metadata. Operators run those from the `heya-media` CLI (`refresh`, `reindex`, `enrich-cast`, `wipe-images`).

## Two endpoints to know

- **`GET /api/v1/search?type=…&q=…`** — merged warm-cache + multi-provider upstream search. Returns canonical `<provider>:<id>` keys you can round-trip.
- **`GET /api/v1/{type}/{id}`** — fetch by external ID (`mbid:…`, `tmdb:…`, `imdb:…`, `apple:…` etc.). Enriches inline on miss.

## Freshness

- A background sweep runs every 24 hours and refreshes docs past a per-kind staleness horizon (days for active artists/series, ~6 months for released movies, ended series, and ended/dead artists).
- A GET for a stale doc kicks off a background refresh; the current request still returns the existing data immediately.
- Lookups for IDs not yet in the database trigger on-demand fetches inline.
info.title: Heya Metadata API

get.description:
Designed for direct round-trip from /api/v1/search — feed the candidate's `id` field into this URL. Supported providers: `mbid`, `apple`, `discogs`, `deezer`.

If the entity isn't in the DB yet, enrichment runs inline (1–60s typical, up to 5 min for popular catalogues). Subsequent calls are instant.

parameters.description: Either a '<provider>:<value>' key (mbid, tmdb, imdb, tvdb, anidb, tvmaze, tvrage, mal, apple, discogs, deezer, ol_work_id) or the artist/title slug as it appears in /heya/<slug>. Slug form looks up existing docs only — no on-miss enrichment, returns 404.

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the HTTP loop is responsive. Use for kubernetes liveness probes / systemd watchdogs.
Returns 200 when every dependency (Mongo, Meili, worker queue, image store) is reachable, 503 otherwise. Use for kubernetes readiness probes / reverse-proxy backend health.
Supported providers: `tmdb`, `imdb`. Enriches inline on miss.

get.description:
Aggregates TheIntroDB and SkipMe.db, normalized to milliseconds, cached server-side (hits re-check every ~30d, misses every ~7d). Returns all candidates with per-source provenance — pick using `duration_ms` proximity to your file's runtime.

Never triggers title enrichment; unenriched titles are queried with the id you pass (tmdb/imdb only).

get.description: Rows from the dispatch table sorted by enrichment recency, excluding people (they churn constantly as cast pre-warm runs and would drown out actual titles). Feed each row's `slug` to `/api/v1/resolve/{slug}` or link to `/{slug}`.

get.description: The detail-page entrypoint: a client landing on `/{slug}` doesn't know the kind up front, so this returns `kind` plus exactly one populated envelope (`movie`/`tv`/`person`/`artist`/`book`) chosen by the doc's home collection. Slug collisions across kinds resolve by the same precedence as the rest of the API (movie > tv > person > artist > book). Existing docs only — no on-miss enrichment.

parameters.description: The canonical slug as it appears in /recent, /browse and /search results
parameters.name: slug

schema.description: The canonical slug as it appears in /recent, /browse and /search results

get.summary: Resolve a bare slug to its kind + full typed doc in one call

get.description:
Fans out to every provider appropriate to `type`, fetches per-hit alt-title detail in parallel, and merges rows that resolve to the same canonical entity (same name across providers) into one — the merged row carries the union of external IDs, sources, and alt_titles. `enriched: true` flags rows that are already in our DB (their slug is then populated and `/api/v1/{type}/{id}` short-circuits).

Every merged row carries `alt_titles[]` — every locale variant, romanization, native-script form, and alias the upstream(s) expose. Use it to match against scanned filenames that don't use the canonical name.

Feed any result's `id` (`<provider>:<value>`) to `/api/v1/{type}/{id}` to fetch the full enriched doc.

Types:
- `artist` → MusicBrainz + Apple + Deezer + Discogs (alt_titles from MB aliases + Discogs namevariations)
- `album` → MusicBrainz release-groups
- `track` → MusicBrainz recordings + Apple + Deezer
- `movie` → TMDB (alt_titles from /alternative_titles + /translations + original_title)
- `tv` → TMDB (same surface as movie)
- `person` → TMDB (alt_titles from /person/{id}.also_known_as)
- `book` → OpenLibrary

get.description:
Combines Last.fm `artist.getSimilar` (collaborative-filtering on Last.fm listening data) and ListenBrainz Labs `similar-artists` (MetaBrainz open listening data, MBID-keyed). Pass `mbid` to hit both sources; `name` only resolves on Last.fm.

Results are returned flat with `source: lastfm | listenbrainz` and a normalized 0..1 `score` within each source. Caller is responsible for any cross-source dedup/ranking.

get.description: Served from an in-process snapshot cache (5-minute TTL, background refresh) — the underlying aggregations are heavy and never run per-request. `generated_at` tells you how fresh the snapshot is.

tags.description: Multi-provider entity discovery — Meili warm cache plus upstream fan-out, deduped by canonical entity. The primary entrypoint for finding things.
tags.name: Search

tags.description: Artist fetch + similarity. Backed by MusicBrainz (canonical), Apple, Deezer, Discogs, Last.fm + ListenBrainz (similarity).
tags.name: Music

tags.description: Movie + TV fetch. TMDB-primary, with TVDB / AniDB / TVMaze / OMDb supplementing.
tags.name: Video

tags.description: Book fetch. OpenLibrary-backed. Read-only — on-demand enrichment isn't implemented yet.
tags.name: Books

tags.description: Community skip segments — intro/recap/credits/preview markers aggregated from TheIntroDB and SkipMe.db, normalized to milliseconds with per-source provenance. Cached server-side; misses re-check weekly as the community databases grow.
tags.name: Segments

tags.description: Read-only views over the local enriched library — homepage feed, browse grid, facets, stats, slug resolution. No upstream calls; everything answers from Mongo / Meilisearch.
tags.name: Library

tags.description: Webui-only helpers. Suppressed from the public spec.
